assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service A with minAvailable=100% as blocking all rolling updates and node drains

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service B with maxUnavailable=0 as preventing any pod disruption and having identical issue to Service A

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Incorrectly identifies Service C as 'risky edge case' rather than safe - it does allow 1 pod disruption (3-2=1)

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service D as safe configuration that allows upgrades while maintaining availability

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Provides fix for Service A suggesting maxUnavailable=1 in both analysis and YAML example

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Provides fix for Service B suggesting maxUnavailable=1 or 25% in both analysis and YAML example

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: No mention of GKE-specific behavior, 1-hour timeout, or force-drain mechanics

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Provides complete YAML manifests and kubectl get/drain commands, but no kubectl patch commands specifically

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Ends with suggestion to test changes in staging environment first
